heading: Gwelup vs Hacketts Gully — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Gwelup, Western Australia and Hacketts Gully, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Gwelup has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Hacketts Gully (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Gwelup or Hacketts Gully safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Gwelup has a flood risk score of 38/100, while Hacketts Gully scores 57/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Gwelup scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. Hacketts Gully scores 35/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Gwelup or Hacketts Gully?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Gwelup has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.33% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
